rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL O'BRIEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Appoint David O¿Brien As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 1. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL O'BRIEN'S MOTION To Appoint David O'Brien As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK CORTEZ VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 2. PLAINTIFF FRANK CORTEZ'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM/JU)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUELO LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT THE C.P. HALL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Hearing is required to address whether plaintiff has verified the responses to special interrogatories. Without a verification, defendant did not shift the burden of producing evidence. The Paro Declaration is not admissible for lack of a proper foundation. If a verification is produced, defendant's special interrogatory responses are factually devoid. The evidence presented by plaintiff does not raise a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 12. PLAINTIFF PAUL VANDER WAERDT's Motion To Amend Complaint. The motion is denied as it relates to Collins. The Court's order of September 22, 2008 dismissed the Mann claims. The Court today grants the motion for summary adjudication as to the APA and Children's House matter. The Bankruptcy Court has not authorized the filing of claims based on misrepresentation by Collins as to malpractice coverage. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT CHARLES COLLINS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary adjudciation is granted. Plaintiff may not recover on the APA and Children's House matters. The Bankruptcy Court granted leave to proceed against Collins to the extent malpractice insurance covered the claims. The record is undisputed that the only malpractice insurance Collins possessed was the Lawyers Matual policy and that policy only covered matters referred to Collins through the Bar Association. Neither the APA nor the Children's House matters were so referred. (UMF 6, 7, 27 and 28). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT CHARLES COLLINS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. it is moot as to Collins and defendant lacks standing re Viltman claims. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN VS. IGP INDUSTRIES, LLC, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Her Complaint
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN'S Motion To Amend Her Complaint -- Duplicate Entry.

rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN VS. IGP INDUSTRIES, LLC, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Bernadette Mcmenamin To Amend Her Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 4. PLAINTIFF BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Amend Her Complaint. The motion is granted in part to add the concealment claim conditioned on defendant being able to bring a motion for summary judgment to address the amended claim. Plaintiff must obtain a continuance of the trial date and consent to reopening discovery. The motion is denied as to the negligent concealment claim for there is no such cause of action. The motion is denied to add the constructive trust claim without prejudice to renew after the summary judgment motion is denied. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CHILDREN'S DAY SCHOOL VS. ROBERT J MCCARTHY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCARTHY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to December 9, 2008. Moving party to provide courtesy copy of moving papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC. VS. GARY ALFRED MIRKOVICH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 6. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden of producing evidence. The Mirkovich Declaration is not admissible for it is based on information and belief, not on personal knowledge. Defendant served a bill of particulars and plaintiff failed to respond. As a consequence, the Frese Declaration does not establish an open book account. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KEAVY NEFF VS. DOES 1 THRU 100, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 3, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT The Roman Cahtolic Archbishop of San Francisco'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrers are sustained in part and overruled in part. The Court finds that the complaint is not barred by CCP section 340.1(a). Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to toll the statute of limitations until April 2005. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the following causes of action: 4th, 5th 6th, 7th and 11th. Plaintiff must clarify these causes of action and focus the claims on defendants' conduct as directed toward plaintiff or other victims at the St. Francis Cabrini School as contrasted with other victims of abuse throughout the Diocese. Plaintiff must clarify what is being alleged in the three fraud causes of action -4th, 5th and 11th causes of action- and make clear the distinction plaintiff apparently seeks to plead. Plaintiff also must provide greater specificity as to when the fraudulent events occurred and who was involved. The demurrers are sustained without leave to amend as to the 8th and 9th causes of action, on opposition having been filed. The demurrers are sustained without leave to amend as to the 10th and 13th causes of action for there is no basis for injunctive relief or a public nuisance based on the facts alleged. The demurrer is overruled as to the 12th cause of action. =(302/PJM/JW)
